---
title: "Practice: Process Diagrams"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
```

# Goal

Practice using a simple diagram to explain an analytic workflow or conceptual structure.

Helpful module reference: `modules/08_flow/02_process-diagrams.qmd`.

# Sketch The Process

Choose one process to explain:

- how raw data become a final figure
- how a sample is filtered
- how variables might relate conceptually
- how a reader should move through a report

# Mermaid Diagram

Create a simple Mermaid diagram. Keep it small enough to explain verbally.

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  A[Start] --> B[Next step]
  B --> C[Final step]
```

# Optional: ggplot Diagram

If you want custom labels or counts, sketch a static diagram with `ggplot2`.

```{r}
# Optional ggplot diagram code here.
```

# Interpret

> TODO: What does the diagram clarify that a normal plot would not?
